title: When On A Summer's Morn
content:
When on a summer's morn I wake,
     And open my two eyes,
    Out to the clear, born-singing rills
     My bird-like spirit flies,

    To hear the Blackbird, Cuckoo, Thrush,
     Or any bird in song;
    And common leaves that hum all day,
     Without a throat or tongue.

    And when Time strikes the hour for sleep,
     Back in my room alone,
    My heart has many a sweet bird's song -
     And one that's all my own.
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Henry Davies, titled "When On A Summer's Morn", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
